intro:
Roppongi, situated in the heart of Minato Ward in central Tokyo, is a vibrant hub of international business and culture. There are 382 stations within a 30-minute commute to Roppongi, expanding to 712 stations within 45 minutes. The average reference rent in the 45-minute commute zone is approximately ¥96,000 per month (based on government housing statistics at the municipal level).

Large-scale complexes such as Roppongi Hills and Tokyo Midtown host foreign-affiliated financial institutions, IT companies, consulting firms, advertising agencies, media companies, and law offices, making the area a magnet for professionals and creative workers. Embassies and art museums and galleries are scattered throughout the neighborhood, giving this business district an unusually high density of cultural facilities and a distinctive cosmopolitan atmosphere.

Two lines serve the area: the Tokyo Metro Hibiya Line and the Toei Oedo Line. The Hibiya Line connects Nakameguro, Ebisu, and Hiroo to the south and Kita-Senju and Minami-Senju to the north, while the Oedo Line branches toward Shinjuku and Nakano, Tsukishima and Ryogoku, and Hikarigaoka. With a transfer, Shibuya, Shinjuku, and Tokyo Station are all generally reachable within around 20 minutes, giving commuters a relatively wide range of route options.

For those looking to keep rent affordable while commuting to Roppongi, strong candidates include the Tokyo Metro Hibiya Line corridor toward Kita-Senju, Minami-Senju, and Minowa; the Toei Oedo Line corridor toward Hikarigaoka, Nerima-Kasugacho, and Shin-Egota; and the corridor toward Ryogoku, Kuramae, and Morishita. All of these allow a direct, transfer-free commute to Roppongi, and the areas around those stations have solid shopping streets and everyday conveniences. On the other hand, nearby neighborhoods such as Azabu-Juban, Akabanebashi, and Hiroo — or Nakameguro and Ebisu — come at a premium but put you just 10 minutes from work.

| Area | Reference Rent (single) | To Roppongi | Main Line |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Azabu-Juban / Akabanebashi area | ¥100,000–130,000 | 5–10 min | Toei Oedo Line |
| Nakameguro / Ebisu area | ¥90,000–120,000 | 10–15 min | Tokyo Metro Hibiya Line |
| Kita-Senju / Minami-Senju area | ¥70,000–90,000 | 25–35 min | Tokyo Metro Hibiya Line |
| Hikarigaoka / Nerima area | ¥70,000–90,000 | 30–40 min | Toei Oedo Line |
| Ryogoku / Kuramae area | ¥80,000–100,000 | 15–25 min | Toei Oedo Line |

Kayoha offers a color-coded commute map for all 1,831 stations in the Greater Tokyo area in 5-minute intervals, along with an AI station recommendation feature that lets you compare 20 candidate stations at once. Combined with reference rent figures, neighborhood profiles, and community ratings, you can compare areas suited to a Roppongi commute side by side on the map.

faq.q: Which areas offer affordable rent within a 30-minute commute to Roppongi?
faq.a: The Tokyo Metro Hibiya Line toward Kita-Senju, Minami-Senju, and Minowa, and the Toei Oedo Line toward Ryogoku, Kuramae, and Morishita, are solid options. These areas offer transfer-free rides of around 20 to 35 minutes, with monthly rents typically in the ¥70,000–100,000 range.

faq.q: Which lines offer the shortest commute to Roppongi?
faq.a: The Tokyo Metro Hibiya Line and the Toei Oedo Line are the two lines that serve Roppongi directly. From the Oedo Line's Azabu-Juban and Akabanebashi direction, and the Hibiya Line's Hiroo and Ebisu direction, the ride takes just 5 to 10 minutes. Including walking-distance options, the range of choices broadens even further.

faq.q: What kind of neighborhood is Roppongi? Is it a good place to live?
faq.a: Roppongi is a business and cultural district anchored by Roppongi Hills and Tokyo Midtown, with a concentration of foreign-affiliated companies, art museums, and embassies. The area stays lively well into the night, and the Azabu-Juban direction has shopping streets and everyday amenities, making it a viable place to live for some.

faq.q: Do popular areas differ between single residents and families commuting to Roppongi?
faq.a: Single residents often prioritize convenience, gravitating toward stations like Azabu-Juban and Akabanebashi on the Oedo Line or Hiroo and Ebisu on the Hibiya Line. Families tend to look further out, toward Hikarigaoka and Nerima on the Oedo Line or Kita-Senju on the Hibiya Line, where larger apartments are easier to find.

faq.q: Which stations are good for catching a first-departure train toward Roppongi?
faq.a: Near the terminal stations of each line — such as Hikarigaoka on the Toei Oedo Line and Kita-Senju or Minami-Senju on the Tokyo Metro Hibiya Line — there are early departures that allow you to ride seated. Check the station pages on Kayoha for details.
